content.problemStatement:
Given arrays of values and weights of items, and a knapsack capacity, find the maximum value achievable by selecting items (allowing fractional parts) such that total weight doesn't exceed capacity.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN and capacity
Second line contains NNN values
Third line contains NNN weights




Output Format

For each test case, print maximum value possible, rounded to 6 decimal places.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains two integers N and C, where N is the number of items and C is the knapsack capacity. The second line contains N integers denoting the values of the items. The third line contains N integers denoting the weights of the items.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum total value that can be obtained, rounded to exactly 6 decimal places.

solution.approach: The fractional knapsack problem is solved greedily. Compute the value‑to‑weight ratio for each item and sort items in decreasing order of this ratio. Then take whole items while the remaining capacity allows; when an item does not fit completely, take the needed fraction of it and stop. This yields the optimal total value because any other selection would replace a higher‑ratio portion with a lower‑ratio one, decreasing the total value. Sorting dominates the complexity.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case due to sorting; overall O(T * N log N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing items and their ratios.
